# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains benchmark results, framework design claims, workflow/mechanism descriptions, ablation findings, and stated limitations for a multi-agent software engineering framework called MetaGPT.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-020
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: metric
citation: MetaGPT paper, Abstract/Introduction
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
MetaGPT reports Pass@1 scores of 85.9% and 87.7% on HumanEval and MBPP.

## Source Excerpt

"To validate the design of MetaGPT, we use publicly available HumanEval ... and MBPP ... for evaluations. Notably, in code generation benchmarks, MetaGPT achieves a new state-of-the-art (SoTA) with 85.9% and 87.7% in Pass@1."

## Note

These are reported benchmark results from the paper.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-020
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: result
citation: MetaGPT paper, Introduction
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The paper claims MetaGPT achieved a 100% task completion rate in its experimental evaluations.

## Source Excerpt

"Remarkably, in our experimental evaluations, MetaGPT achieves a 100 % percent 100 100\% 100 % task completion rate, demonstrating the robustness and efficiency (time and token costs) of our design."

## Note

The source states the 100% task completion claim in the introduction, but the exact task set and evaluation framing for this figure are not specified in that sentence.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-020
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: claim-support
citation: MetaGPT paper, Introduction
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The paper attributes MetaGPT's error reduction to encoding standardized operating procedures into prompt sequences and having agents verify intermediate results.

## Source Excerpt

"MetaGPT encodes Standardized Operating Procedures (SOPs) into prompt sequences for more streamlined workflows, thus allowing agents with human-like domain expertise to verify intermediate results and reduce errors."

## Note

This is the paper's stated mechanism claim for why the framework reduces errors.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-020
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: claim-support
citation: MetaGPT paper, Introduction
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The paper claims that requiring structured intermediate outputs significantly increases target code generation success rate.

## Source Excerpt

"MetaGPT requires agents to generate structured outputs, such as high-quality requirements documents, design artifacts, flowcharts, and interface specifications. The use of intermediate structured outputs significantly increases the success rate of target code generation."

## Note

The statement is a mechanism-supporting design claim rather than a standalone quantified result in this passage.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-020
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: method
citation: MetaGPT paper, Sec. 3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
MetaGPT defines five software-company roles: Product Manager, Architect, Project Manager, Engineer, and QA Engineer.

## Source Excerpt

"We define five roles in our software company: Product Manager, Architect, Project Manager, Engineer, and QA Engineer, as shown in Figure 1."

## Note

This is a core workflow design detail of the framework.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-020
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: method
citation: MetaGPT paper, Sec. 3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
MetaGPT uses a shared message pool so agents can publish structured messages and retrieve needed information directly.

## Source Excerpt

"We introduce a shared message pool that allows all agents to exchange messages directly. These agents not only publish their structured messages in the pool but also access messages from other entities transparently. Any agent can directly retrieve required information from the shared pool, eliminating the need to inquire about other agents and await their responses."

## Note

This is a stated communication mechanism in the framework.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-020
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: method
citation: MetaGPT paper, Sec. 3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
MetaGPT uses a subscription mechanism so agents select role-relevant information and act only after prerequisite dependencies are received.

## Source Excerpt

"We offer a simple and effective solution- subscription mechanism ... Instead of relying on dialogue, agents utilize role-specific interests to extract relevant information. They can select information to follow based on their role profiles. In practical implementations, an agent activates its action only after receiving all its prerequisite dependencies."

## Note

This is presented as the framework's approach to limiting information overload.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-020
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: method
citation: MetaGPT paper, Sec. 3.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
MetaGPT's executable feedback loop continues iterative testing and debugging until tests pass or three retries are reached.

## Source Excerpt

"To obtain additional information, the Engineer writes and executes the corresponding unit test cases, and subsequently receives the test results. If satisfactory, additional development tasks are initiated. Otherwise the Engineer debugs the code before resuming programming. This iterative testing process continues until the test is passed or a maximum of 3 retries is reached."

## Note

This is the paper's concrete description of the executable feedback procedure.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-020
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: metric
citation: MetaGPT paper, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
On SoftwareDev, Table 1 reports executability scores of 2.25 for ChatDev, 3.67 for MetaGPT without feedback, and 3.75 for MetaGPT.

## Source Excerpt

"Table 1: The statistical analysis on SoftwareDev.
(A) Executability 2.25 3.67 3.75"

## Note

These are tabulated comparative results on the SoftwareDev benchmark.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-020
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: metric
citation: MetaGPT paper, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
On SoftwareDev, Table 1 reports running times of 762 seconds for ChatDev, 503 seconds for MetaGPT without feedback, and 541 seconds for MetaGPT.

## Source Excerpt

"Table 1: The statistical analysis on SoftwareDev.
(B) Cost#1: Running Times (s) 762 503 541"

## Note

The table reports MetaGPT without feedback as faster than both ChatDev and MetaGPT with feedback on this metric.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-020
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: metric
citation: MetaGPT paper, Sec. 4.4
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Adding executable feedback is reported to improve Pass@1 by 4.2% on HumanEval and 5.4% on MBPP.

## Source Excerpt

"As shown in Figure 4, adding executable feedback into MetaGPT leads to a significant improvement of 4.2% and 5.4% in Pass@1 on HumanEval and MBPP, respectively."

## Note

This is a quantified ablation result tied directly to the executable feedback mechanism.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-020
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: incident
citation: MetaGPT paper, Appendix D.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The paper states that the current system cannot fully handle some scenarios such as UI and front-end because relevant agents and multimodal tools have not yet been incorporated.

## Source Excerpt

"At present, our system cannot fully cater to specific scenarios, such as UI and front-end, as we have yet to incorporate such agents and multimodal tools."

## Note

This is an explicit limitation that bounds the scope of the reported system capabilities.
---

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-020
evidence_id: evidence-06

citation: MetaGPT paper, Sec. 3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
MetaGPT uses a shared message pool so agents can publish structured messages and retrieve needed information directly.

## Source Excerpt

"We introduce a shared message pool that allows all agents to exchange messages directly. These agents not only publish their structured messages in the pool but also access messages from other entities transparently. Any agent can directly retrieve required information from the shared pool, eliminating the need to inquire about other agents and await their responses."

## Note

This is a stated communication mechanism in the framework.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-020
evidence_id: evidence-07

citation: MetaGPT paper, Sec. 3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
MetaGPT uses a subscription mechanism so agents select role-relevant information and act only after prerequisite dependencies are received.

## Source Excerpt

"We offer a simple and effective solution- subscription mechanism ... Instead of relying on dialogue, agents utilize role-specific interests to extract relevant information. They can select information to follow based on their role profiles. In practical implementations, an agent activates its action only after receiving all its prerequisite dependencies."

## Note

This is presented as the framework's approach to limiting information overload.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-020
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: method
citation: MetaGPT paper, Sec. 3.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00352
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
MetaGPT's executable feedback loop continues iterative testing and debugging until tests pass or three retries are reached.

## Source Excerpt

"To obtain additional information, the Engineer writes and executes the corresponding unit test cases, and subsequently receives the test results. If satisfactory, additional development tasks are initiated. Otherwise the Engineer debugs the code before resuming programming. This iterative testing process continues until the test is passed or a maximum of 3 retries is reached."

## Note

This is the paper's concrete description of the executable feedback procedure.
```
